tester.role: automated fontTools inspection with human result review

input: BricolageGrotesque[opsz,wdth,wght].ttf from Google Fonts commit b9f6c712059d72742282ebdf06eadfc264c827f3.
steps: Download the exact TTF from the pinned Google Fonts source commit.
Inspect name, version, OS/2 fsType, fvar axes, cmap, GSUB/GPOS feature tags, and required currency/punctuation code points.
Compare advance widths of base digits zero through nine.
Inspect Google Fonts family directory for a separate italic file.
expected: File parses cleanly; identity and version are readable; embedding flag, axes, binary coverage, OpenType features, currency glyphs, numeral widths, and italic-file presence can be recorded.
actual: Parsed as Bricolage Grotesque version 1.001. SHA-256 matched candidate record. fsType was 0. Optical-size axis was 12–96, weight 200–800, and width 75–100. Inspected coverage supports a Latin-only candidate claim. INR, USD, EUR, GBP, JPY, bullet, en dash, and em dash were present. Base digits were proportional; a tabular figure feature was present. No separate italic file appeared in the current Google Fonts family directory.

artifacts.description: Candidate metadata derived from this inspection

notes: Pass covers binary inspection only. It does not establish language support beyond reviewed Latin evidence, visual suitability, application behaviour, static file choice, or human typography review.
